coverage: comprehensive — 3 samples for each of all 24 qualities
verdict: This tiny template model performs solidly: every one of the 72 outputs is grammatical (lowercase but clean), names the requested quality, and stays general/professional with zero romantic or creepy content. The previously-known impact-mismatch bug is largely fixed (e.g. no more 'kindness -> deadline reachable'), with appropriate impacts in ~22 of 24 qualities; a few weak fits remain. Variety is the main remaining limitation, with several qualities repeating the identical impact clause across all three samples.
qualities_with_issues: patience: 'made everyone feel welcome' fits warmth/care more than patience
generosity: 'saved us a lot of time' is a weak fit; better maps to helping the team
integrity: 'made things clearer' reads as clarity rather than integrity
humility / rigor / warmth / kindness: appropriate but identical impact across all 3 samples (low within-quality variety)
improvement_over_previous: overall 0.78 -> 0.89; appropriateness now scored (0.88) after mapping impacts to fitting qualities; per-quality accuracy 0.92.
note: LLM-as-judge qualitative benchmark, recorded honestly alongside the deterministic Python metrics in benchmarks.json. Not a capability-class claim.
